## Conflict / Question / Contradiction Protocol

Three append-only JSONL channels at `working/wiki/pass2-buckets/`. Always append; never overwrite.

### `questions-for-matt.jsonl` — when human input is needed

Use when:
- The candidate represents a relationship not in the locked vocabulary AND you think it should be (file a `vocabulary-gap` question with ≥3 example sentences).
- The candidate is genuinely undecidable across all four decision types (file a `prose-edge-other` question).
- The source or target node file is missing or unparseable (file an `infrastructure` question).

Schema:
```json
{"question_id": "q-<UTC-DATE>-<bucket-slug>-NNN", "bucket_id": "<bucket-id>", "agent": "prose-edge-classifier", "type": "vocabulary-gap|prose-edge-other|infrastructure", "text": "<one paragraph>", "context": {"source": "...", "target": "...", "snippet": "..."}, "blocking": false, "asked_at": "<UTC ISO8601>", "resolved_at": null, "resolution": null}
```

### `pass1-contradictions.jsonl` — when wiki contradicts a chapter extraction

If your reading of the source prose contradicts what Pass 1 chapter extractions said about the same entity, file a row. The `contradiction-surfacer` agent will pick it up. Don't try to resolve the contradiction yourself.

### `conflicts.jsonl` — when two prose snippets disagree about the same edge

If candidate A says edge X→Y is `SPOUSE_OF` and candidate B (different snippet) says X→Y is `LOVER_OF`, file a conflict row. Emit your best-judgment edge in `prose-edges/`, but log the conflict.

## Definition of Done — per input file

You exit successfully for an input candidates file when:
- Every candidate row in the input has a corresponding decision row in the matching output file (or candidate count was zero, in which case no output file is created).
- Every decision row carries the `candidate_kind` discriminator (matching the input).
- All emit_edge decisions use only the locked master vocabulary (~159 edge types in architecture.md § "Edge Types (Relationship Categories)").
- For `comention` emit_edge decisions: a direction was picked (`a_to_b`, `b_to_a`, or `symmetric`).
- All structured-channel rows you wanted to file are appended.
- You produced no output anywhere outside the input's matching `prose-edges/` sibling dir and the three append-only channels.

The launcher then runs `wiki-pass2-promote-prose-edges.py`, which reads your JSONL and appends accepted edges to nodes under a `## Edges (prose-derived)` subheading — keeping infobox edges immutable. For comention edges, the promoter writes the edge into the `source` node (per the direction the classifier picked) and adds an `evidence_chapter:` citation to the qualifier. You do not perform that promotion.

14	## Identity
15	
16	Maege Mormont is a character.human from the AWOIAF wiki.
17	
18	## Edges
19	
20	- HOLDS_TITLE: Lady of Bear Island (track_b: Title)
21	- SWORN_TO: House Mormont (track_b: Allegiance)
22	- CULTURE_OF: Northmen (track_b: Culture)

28	- PARENT_OF: Jorelle Mormont (track_b: Issue)
29	- PARENT_OF: Lyanna Mormont (track_b: Issue)
30	
31	## Origins
32	
33	Maege inherited the title of head of House Mormont and ruler of Bear Island after her brother's only heir, Jorah Mormont, fled to the [Free Cities](wiki:Free_Cities) to escape justice for unlawfully selling poachers into [slavery](wiki:Slavery) to pay for his debts. She worked hard to regain the family's noble name, and to help the estate recover from the state of near-poverty Jorah left behind.(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-RpgBook_7-0)
34	
35	Maege has five daughters: her eldest and heir, [Dacey](wiki:Dacey_Mormont); and [Alysane](wiki:Alysane_Mormont), [Lyra](wiki:Lyra_Mormont), [Jorelle](wiki:Jorelle_Mormont), and [Lyanna](wiki:Lyanna_Mormont). She has no sons.
36	
37	No one knows who the father of Maege's children is, or even if she ever married.(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-ssm_8-0) There are rumors that she beds with a bear;(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-Racok6.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0)(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-Racok8.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0) her daughter, Alysane, smilingly shares the same rumor, claiming that Mormont women are [skinchangers](wiki:Skinchanger) who turn into bears and find mates in the woods.(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-Radwd42.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0) Maege prefers to keep her own counsel.(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-ssm_8-1) Nonetheless, all of Maege's daughters carry the surname "Mormont", and none appear to be considered [bastards](wiki:Bastard). It is possible that Maege's husband came from a family of lower nobility than the Mormonts, or no nobility at all, in which case a woman can prefer to retain her own surname for herself and for her children.(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-ssm_8-2)
38	
39	## Appearances & Description
40	
41	> *See also: [Images of Maege Mormont](wiki:Category:Images_of_Maege_Mormont)*
42	
43	Maege is a short, stout, grey-haired woman, and a fierce warrior. She dresses in patched ringmail, and her favored weapon is a spiked mace.(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-Ragot53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0)(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-Rasos14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0) She is dedicated to the [old gods](wiki:Old_gods), and loyal to [House Stark](wiki:House_Stark). According to her brother, Jeor, she is stubborn, short-tempered, and willful.(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-Ragot70.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0)
44	
45	## Narrative Arc
46	
47	### A Game of Thrones
48	
49	Lady Maege and her heir, [Dacey](wiki:Dacey_Mormont), lead their forces to [Winterfell](wiki:Winterfell) when [Robb Stark](wiki:Robb_Stark) calls his banners to ride south against the [Lannisters](wiki:House_Lannister).(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-Ragot53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-1) When the northern host splits at [the Twins](wiki:The_Twins), Maege continues south in Robb's host. Her forces signal the springing of the trap at the [Battle of the Whispering Wood](wiki:Battle_of_the_Whispering_Wood).(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-Ragot63.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0) After Lords [Greatjon Umber](wiki:Greatjon_Umber) and [Rickard Karstark](wiki:Rickard_Karstark) proclaim Robb to be [King in the North](wiki:King_in_the_North) at [Riverrun](wiki:Riverrun), Maege hails Robb as "King of Winter".(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-Ragot71.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0)
50	
51	### A Clash of Kings
52	
53	After the [Battle of Oxcross](wiki:Battle_of_Oxcross), Lady Mormont captures thousands of cattle in the [Westerlands](wiki:Westerlands) and drives them towards the [Riverlands](wiki:Riverlands). It is not stated whether or not she manages to succeed in this task, as the passage back to the Riverlands is still held by the Lannisters.(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-Racok39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0)
54	
55	### A Storm of Swords

## Origins

Maege inherited the title of head of House Mormont and ruler of Bear Island after her brother's only heir, Jorah Mormont, fled to the [Free Cities](wiki:Free_Cities) to escape justice for unlawfully selling poachers into [slavery](wiki:Slavery) to pay for his debts. She worked hard to regain the family's noble name, and to help the estate recover from the state of near-poverty Jorah left behind.(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-RpgBook_7-0)

Maege has five daughters: her eldest and heir, [Dacey](wiki:Dacey_Mormont); and [Alysane](wiki:Alysane_Mormont), [Lyra](wiki:Lyra_Mormont), [Jorelle](wiki:Jorelle_Mormont), and [Lyanna](wiki:Lyanna_Mormont). She has no sons.

No one knows who the father of Maege's children is, or even if she ever married.(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-ssm_8-0) There are rumors that she beds with a bear;(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-Racok6.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0)(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-Racok8.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0) her daughter, Alysane, smilingly shares the same rumor, claiming that Mormont women are [skinchangers](wiki:Skinchanger) who turn into bears and find mates in the woods.(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-Radwd42.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0) Maege prefers to keep her own counsel.(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-ssm_8-1) Nonetheless, all of Maege's daughters carry the surname "Mormont", and none appear to be considered [bastards](wiki:Bastard). It is possible that Maege's husband came from a family of lower nobility than the Mormonts, or no nobility at all, in which case a woman can prefer to retain her own surname for herself and for her children.(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-ssm_8-2)

## Appearances & Description

> *See also: [Images of Maege Mormont](wiki:Category:Images_of_Maege_Mormont)*

Maege is a short, stout, grey-haired woman, and a fierce warrior. She dresses in patched ringmail, and her favored weapon is a spiked mace.(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-Ragot53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0)(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-Rasos14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0) She is dedicated to the [old gods](wiki:Old_gods), and loyal to [House Stark](wiki:House_Stark). According to her brother, Jeor, she is stubborn, short-tempered, and willful.(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-Ragot70.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0)

## Narrative Arc

### A Game of Thrones

Lady Maege and her heir, [Dacey](wiki:Dacey_Mormont), lead their forces to [Winterfell](wiki:Winterfell) when [Robb Stark](wiki:Robb_Stark) calls his banners to ride south against the [Lannisters](wiki:House_Lannister).(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-Ragot53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-1) When the northern host splits at [the Twins](wiki:The_Twins), Maege continues south in Robb's host. Her forces signal the springing of the trap at the [Battle of the Whispering Wood](wiki:Battle_of_the_Whispering_Wood).(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-Ragot63.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0) After Lords [Greatjon Umber](wiki:Greatjon_Umber) and [Rickard Karstark](wiki:Rickard_Karstark) proclaim Robb to be [King in the North](wiki:King_in_the_North) at [Riverrun](wiki:Riverrun), Maege hails Robb as "King of Winter".(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-Ragot71.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0)

### A Clash of Kings

After the [Battle of Oxcross](wiki:Battle_of_Oxcross), Lady Mormont captures thousands of cattle in the [Westerlands](wiki:Westerlands) and drives them towards the [Riverlands](wiki:Riverlands). It is not stated whether or not she manages to succeed in this task, as the passage back to the Riverlands is still held by the Lannisters.(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-Racok39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0)

### A Storm of Swords

Lady Maege is among the northern lords who accompanies King Robb Stark north from [Riverrun](wiki:Riverrun) to attend the wedding of [Edmure Tully](wiki:Edmure_Tully) at [the Twins](wiki:The_Twins). At [Hag's Mire](wiki:Hag's_Mire), however, she and [Galbart Glover](wiki:Galbart_Glover) are ordered by Robb to travel to [Seagard](wiki:Seagard). There they are to take separate ships from Lord [Jason Mallister](wiki:Jason_Mallister) to sail up [the Neck](wiki:The_Neck) and to search for [Greywater Watch](wiki:Greywater_Watch) as envoys to the [crannogmen](wiki:Crannogmen), in preparation for Robb's planned attack on [ironborn](wiki:Ironborn)-occupied [Moat Cailin](wiki:Moat_Cailin). The letters they carry will contain false commands for northern lords, however, in case they are captured by enemies.(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-Rasos45.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0) Maege's heir, Dacey, is one of those killed at the Twins during the [Red Wedding](wiki:Red_Wedding).(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-Rasos51.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-0)

### A Dance with Dragons

[Alysane Mormont](wiki:Alysane_Mormont) mentions her mother, Maege, when [Asha Greyjoy](wiki:Asha_Greyjoy) asks about her siblings. Maege is with two of her other daughters, [Lyra](wiki:Lyra_Mormont) and [Jory](wiki:Jorelle_Mormont), though it is not said where, while her youngest, [Lyanna](wiki:Lyanna_Mormont) is back on Bear Island.(wiki:Maege_Mormont.cite_ref-Radwd42.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-1)

## Identity

King in the North is a royal title held by the ruling monarchs of the North — the vast region of Westeros north of the Neck — for thousands of years before Aegon's Conquest. The title originated after the Long Night and the founding of Winterfell, with House Stark of Winterfell becoming its primary and near-perpetual holders. In the age before the Targaryens, the Starks were called Kings of Winter (an older variant of the title) and held sway over the North for roughly eight thousand years since the Age of Heroes, beginning with legendary figures such as Brandon the Builder. The title's holders gradually subdued rival claimants, including the Red Kings of House Bolton to the east and the Barrow Kings to the south, consolidating the North under Stark rule. During the coming of the Andals, the Kings of Winter were the only kingdom in Westeros to successfully turn back Andal invasion, repelling them at Moat Cailin and the eastern shores. Notable individual kings include Theon Stark, who defeated the Andal warlord Argos Sevenstar at the Battle of the Weeping Water and then sailed to burn Andal villages in Andalos; Jon Stark, who drove raiders from the White Knife and built the Wolf's Den; and Torrhen Stark, the last independent King in the North, who surrendered his crown at the Trident to Aegon I Targaryen rather than face dragonflame, earning the epithet "the King Who Knelt." After Torrhen's submission, the Starks became Lords of Winterfell and the title fell dormant for nearly three centuries, with the Inn of the Kneeling Man marking the spot where Torrhen bent the knee.

The title was revived during the War of the Five Kings when, following Eddard Stark's execution by King Joffrey, the assembled northmen and river lords at Riverrun proclaimed Robb Stark King in the North and (separately) King of the Trident. Greatjon Umber was the first to declare Robb king, rousing the others with the argument that the North should rule itself again, as the Targaryen dragons were gone. Robb's smiths forged him a crown modeled on the ancient crowns of the Kings of Winter — a hammered bronze circlet incised with runes of the First Men, topped with nine iron spikes shaped like longswords. His reign was cut short at the Red Wedding, where he was slain by Roose Bolton at the Twins; his direwolf Grey Wind's head was sewn onto his body as a final insult to the King in the North. In the aftermath, House Bolton was named Warden of the North by the Iron Throne, effectively extinguishing the title again. Loyalist sentiment persisted among northern houses: Lyanna Mormont of Bear Island responded to Stannis Baratheon's demand for homage with the message "Bear Island knows no king but the King in the North, whose name is STARK."
